title: Valve Authority Range for HVAC Coil Applications
summary: Valve authority between 30 to 50% is adequate for HVAC coil applications with 2:1 branch-to-riser pressure drop ratios. Higher Δt coils require less valve authority.
evidence_quote: Authorities in the range of 30 to 50% (for piping systems with 2:1 branch-to-riser pressure drop ratios) are adequate for HVAC coil applications, with higher Δt coils requiring less valve authority.

title: Minimum Valve Authority to Avoid
summary: Valve authority less than 30% should be avoided as it causes a flow characteristic shift, making an equal-percentage valve appear more linear.
evidence_quote: Authorities less than 30% should be avoided because they lead to a flow characteristic shift that makes the equal-percentage valve appear to be more linear in nature.

title: Control Valve Sizing Basis
summary: Control valves should be sized based on the required valve coefficient Cv for the required pressure drop and flow.
evidence_quote: Control valves should be sized on the basis of the required valve coefficient Cv for the required pressure drop and flow.

title: Coil Entering Air Temperature Rating Range
summary: Dry-surface and dehumidifying coils are rated with entering air dry-bulb temperatures from 18 to 38°C and wet-bulb temperatures from 15 to 30°C.
evidence_quote:
Entering air dry-bulb temperature: 18 to 38°C
Entering air wet-bulb temperature: 15 to 30°C

title: Coil Air Face Velocity Rating Range
summary: Coils are rated for air face velocities between 1 and 4 m/s, with dehumidifying coils for comfort applications often selected in the 2.0 to 2.5 m/s range.

title: Chilled-Water Temperature Rating Range
summary: Entering chilled-water temperature for coil ratings ranges from 1.5 to 18°C.

title: Water Velocity Rating Range for Coils
summary: Water velocity for coil ratings ranges from 0.3 to 2.4 m/s, and for ethylene glycol solution from 0.3 to 1.8 m/s.
evidence_quote:
Water velocity: 0.3 to 2.4 m/s
For ethylene glycol solution: 0.3 to 1.8 m/s

title: Dehumidifying Coil Sensible Heat Ratio Range
summary: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies from about 0.6 to 1.0 (60 to 100% sensible heat).
evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0

title: Low-Temperature Heating System Supply Temperatures
summary: Low-temperature heating systems are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.
evidence_quote: They are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.

title: Historical US Hot-Water Heating Design
summary: Historically, US hot-water heating systems were designed for a 93°C supply water temperature and an 11 K temperature drop.
evidence_quote: In the United States, hot-water heating systems were historically designed for a 93°C supply water temperature and an 11 K temperature drop.

title: Valve Authority for Three-Way Valve Circuit
summary: For a three-way valve circuit, the pressure drop in the bypass balancing valve should be set to equal that in the coil (load).
evidence_quote: The pressure drop in the bypass balancing valve in the three-way valve circuit should be set to equal that in the coil (load).

title: Coil Freezing Prevention Threshold
summary: Freezing of dehumidifying coil surfaces occurs when the refrigerant evaporating temperature at the coil outlet is below –4 to –2°C saturated.
evidence_quote: Freezing does not occur with standard coils for comfort installations unless the refrigerant evaporating temperature at the coil outlet is below –4 to –2°C saturated

title: Airflow Resistance Comparison
summary: A completely dry coil removing only sensible heat offers approximately one-third less resistance to airflow than a dehumidifying coil removing both sensible and latent heat.
evidence_quote: A completely dry coil, removing only sensible heat, offers approximately one-third less resistance to airflow than a dehumidifying coil removing both sensible and latent heat.

title: Hydrophilic Coil Pressure Drop Reduction
summary: Hydrophilic aluminum fin surface coatings can reduce air pressure drop across a new coil by as much as 30% compared to an untreated coil.
evidence_quote: Tests have shown as much as a 30% reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil as opposed a new untreated coil.

title: Variable-Speed Pump Control Stability
summary: Variable-speed circulating pumps can provide stable control of coil discharge temperature, with stability on the order of ±0.28 K.
evidence_quote: Stability of the controlled discharge temperature of the coil was on the order of ±0.28 K.
